vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) and P2 (Mexican Mint) are not visible as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is absent. The desk surface contains only soil-filled pots and a white cylindrical object (likely a sensor or container).

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Soil appears dark and potentially moist, but lacks any vegetative growth.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of a white cylindrical object (sensor/bottle) and a black sensor stake in the yellow pot.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: The final image is a total black-out, indicating a failure of the light source or camera obstruction.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: From EARLIEST to T-1, the pots remained consistently devoid of visible plant life. The transition to CURRENT shows a complete loss of visual data (black frame), suggesting a catastrophic failure of the monitoring environment.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. There is no evidence of the registered plants. The environment is effectively a collection of empty pots with soil.
vision_report.anomalies: The primary anomaly is the total absence of the registered plants across all frames, suggesting they were either never present, removed, or have completely decomposed/withered below the soil line. The final image is a total blackout.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete absence of the expected botanical specimens. The pots contain only soil and technical apparatus. The sequence concludes with a total loss of visual input, rendering the current state of the biome unobservable.
